source: British Council–EAQUALS Core Inventory for General English, 2nd ed. 2015

cards.title: Greetings & introducing yourself
cards.goal: Can greet people, introduce him/herself and respond to introductions.
grammar: to be incl. questions and negatives
exponents: This is Mary. Pleased to meet you.
cards.evidence: Handle a first meeting: greet, introduce self (name, origin, where you live), respond to the other person's introduction, close politely.

cards.title: Exchanging personal information
cards.goal: Can ask for and give personal information: address, nationality, age, family, job, hobbies.
cards.evidence: Ask five personal questions and answer five, including one about family and one about work/hobbies; complete a hotel check-in or landing-card form.

cards.title: My people & my things
cards.goal: Can say what he/she has and talk simply about family and possessions.
cards.evidence: Present three people, three things: introduce three family members or friends and three possessions using have got and possessives correctly.

cards.title: Likes & dislikes
cards.goal: Can say what he/she likes, loves and hates.
cards.evidence: Six true like/dislike sentences across two topics, at least three with verb + -ing.

cards.title: Daily life & routines
cards.goal: Can describe habits, routines and daily life in simple sentences.
grammar: adverbs of frequency: always, sometimes, never
cards.evidence: Describe a typical day in six or more sentences, including two other people (3rd person) and two frequency adverbs.

cards.title: What's happening now
cards.goal: Can say what he/she and others are doing at the moment.
cards.evidence: Picture narration: describe what five people in a busy scene are doing, correctly formed.

cards.title: Where things are
cards.goal: Can say and ask where things and places are.
cards.evidence: Describe a room or simple map (five sentences with there is/are + place prepositions), then answer two where-is questions.

cards.title: Describing simply
cards.goal: Can describe things, clothes and appearance with basic adjectives.
cards.evidence: Describe three items (one with this/that, one with colour + size, one with a comparative) so the listener can pick them out of a set.

cards.title: What I can do
cards.evidence: Six true can/can't sentences about self and one other person; ask the listener two can-you questions.

cards.title: Numbers & prices
cards.goal: Can understand and use numbers, quantities and prices.
grammar: how much/how many with very common countable/uncountable nouns
cards.evidence: Ask the cost of three things, understand three spoken prices at varied speed, state two quantities about own life.

cards.title: Time & dates
cards.goal: Can tell the time, understand times and use days/months in arrangements.
cards.evidence: Answer what-time for five clock times; say three true sentences about own schedule using in/on/at correctly.

cards.title: Finding the way
cards.goal: Can ask for, understand and give simple directions.
exponents: Go to the end of the street and turn right
The hotel is on the left
cards.evidence: Map task both directions: follow spoken directions to a target, then guide the teacher to a different target.

cards.title: Getting what you want
cards.goal: Can ask for everyday goods and services: order, buy, and make simple requests in shops, cafés and travel situations.
lexis: things in the town, shops and shopping
cards.evidence: Two roleplays from the On Holiday scenario (e.g. café order + buying a ticket) without L1, including one I'd-like and one could-I-have.

cards.title: First steps into the past
cards.goal: Can make simple statements about past events and states.
cards.evidence: Five true sentences about last weekend or a past holiday: two with was/were, three with other past verbs. No narrative structure required.

cards.title: First plans
cards.goal: Can say what he/she is going to do.
cards.evidence: Three true going-to sentences about this week plus one going-to question to the listener.

cards.title: Connecting ideas
cards.goal: Can link words and simple sentences with basic connectors.
cards.evidence: Speak or write a postcard with six sentences containing at least two and, two but, two because, all used correctly.
cards.note: This is the CEFR's own A1 coherence criterion.
